title: Transactional Outbox, Relay, Inbox, Sagas, and Compensation · TypeScript Build Room
summary: A messaging pairing room predicts publisher, exchange, queue, consumer, ownership, and failure transitions before assembling the first outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol for transactional outbox, relay, inbox, sagas, and compensation. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Enumerate database-first, publish-first, timeout, crash, confirm-loss, rollback, and duplicate failure windows for a dual write.
Store domain mutation and immutable outbox message identity, type, payload, destination intent, and availability atomically.
Claim bounded outbox rows, publish with backpressure and confirms, mark completion safely, and recover ambiguous attempts through stable identity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Workshop Dual Write Failure Matrix
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop messaging team handles case 1813 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to close the local dual-write gap and preserve confirm or external-effect ambiguity; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then exhaust the retry age before the attempt count reaches its maximum. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

hints: For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Saga Compensation, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Saga Compensation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Saga Compensation, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A workshop messaging team handles case 9244 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to close the local dual-write gap and preserve confirm or external-effect ambiguity; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then change one routing segment and prove the exact destination set. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Saga Compensation, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A compensation always restores the world to its exact state before the original action.

stimulus.title: Saga Compensation failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A compensation always restores the world to its exact state before the original action.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, apply Saga Compensation without its stated preconditions, omit the outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Saga Compensation for outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Saga Compensation condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Saga Compensation output and hide the violated outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Saga Compensation.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Saga Compensation
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Saga Compensation: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop messaging team handles case 5101 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to close the local dual-write gap and preserve confirm or external-effect ambiguity; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Saga Compensation.
steps.why: Retrieving Saga Compensation through the concrete outbox, relay, inbox, and compensation protocol case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend the transferred model · Workshop Saga Compensation, keep Saga Compensation connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Design saga state, commands, events, timeouts, idempotent steps, compensations, irreversibility, operator intervention, and audit evidence.
